description: What will be the output of the following code?
explanation: The variable a is initialized with value 5. The cout statement prints the value directly. No modification or operation is performed on a before printing.

description: What will be the output?
explanation: Both operands are integers so integer division happens. The fractional part is discarded, so 5 divided by 2 becomes 2.

description: Which header is required for using cout?
explanation: The iostream header provides input output stream objects like cout and cin. Without including it, the program will not compile.

description: What is the output?
explanation: Local variables are not initialized by default. So variable a contains a garbage value which gets printed.

description: Which operator gives address of a variable?
explanation: The ampersand operator returns the memory address of a variable. It is commonly used with pointers.

description: Which keyword is used to declare constant variables?
explanation: The const keyword is used to define variables whose values cannot be changed after initialization.

description: Which is a valid variable name?
explanation: Variable names cannot start with digits and cannot contain hyphens. _value is valid because it starts with underscore.

description: What is the output?
explanation: The exact string Hello is printed because cout outputs the string literal as it is.

description: Which loop runs at least once?
explanation: The do while loop executes the body first and then checks the condition, so it always runs at least once.

description: What will be output?
explanation: Multiplication has higher precedence than addition so 3 multiplied by 2 equals 6, then 2 plus 6 equals 8.

description: What will be output?
explanation: In C++ relational operators return 1 for true and 0 for false. Since 5 is greater than 3, result is true which is printed as 1.

description: Which data type is used for decimal values?
explanation: float and double are used for decimal values. Among the options, float is correct.

description: What is the output?
explanation: Character A has ASCII value 65. When printed as char, it shows A.

description: Which symbol is used to end a statement?
explanation: In C++ every statement ends with a semicolon which tells the compiler that the statement is complete.

description: Which operator is used for modulus?
explanation: The modulus operator returns the remainder after division.

description: What is output?
explanation: Pre increment increases value first then returns it. So a becomes 4 and is printed.

description: Which keyword is used for decision making?
explanation: if is used to check condition and execute code accordingly.

description: Which function is entry point of program?
explanation: main function is where execution of program starts.

description: What will be output?
explanation: a is assigned value 10 and printed directly.

description: Which keyword is used to return value?
explanation: return statement is used to return value from function.

description: What will be the output of the following code involving operator precedence?
explanation: Multiplication has higher precedence than addition, so 4 multiplied by 5 gives 20, then 20 plus 4 gives 24.

description: What will be printed considering implicit type conversion?
explanation: Both operands are integers, so integer division happens first resulting in 2, then it is assigned to float as 2.0.

description: What will be output due to short circuit evaluation?
explanation: Since the first condition is false, the second part of logical AND is not evaluated, preventing division by zero.

description: What will be the output demonstrating variable shadowing?
explanation: Inner variable shadows outer variable, so 20 is printed.

description: What will be the output when using pre increment inside expression?
explanation: Pre increment increases value before use. So a becomes 6, then 6 plus 2 equals 8.

description: What will be output involving pointer dereferencing?
explanation: Pointer p stores address of a. Dereferencing p gives value stored at that address which is 5.

description: What will be output of this loop logic?
explanation: Loop runs while i<=3 so prints 0 1 2 3.

description: What will be output of bitwise OR?
explanation: Binary OR of 1 and 2 is 3.

description: What will be the output considering undefined behavior due to multiple modifications?
explanation: The variable a is modified twice in the same expression without a sequence point. C++ does not define the order of evaluation here, leading to undefined behavior.

description: What will be printed when using pointer arithmetic?
explanation: Pointer arithmetic moves by size of data type. p+1 moves to next integer, so value becomes 20.

description: What will be output with static variable in function?
explanation: Static variables retain their value between function calls. First call prints 1, second call increments and prints 2.

description: What will be output due to array decay to pointer?
explanation: When passed to function, array decays into pointer, so sizeof gives size of pointer not full array.

description: What will be output with reference variable?
explanation: Reference variable refers to same memory. Changing r changes a, so a becomes 10.

description: What will be output of recursion base case?
explanation: Function returns 1 when base condition is met.

description: What will be output of this tricky loop?
explanation: Condition i++<4 uses post increment so comparison uses old value. Loop runs 4 times.

description: What will be output of this expression?
explanation: Comparison returns boolean but cout prints it as 1 for true.

description: What will be output of this pointer comparison?
explanation: Both pointers point to same array base address so comparison is true.

description: What will be output with const pointer?
explanation: Pointer to const cannot modify value it points to, so assignment causes compilation error.

description: What will be the output of accessing first element of array?
explanation: Array indexing starts from 0. So arr[0] refers to the first element which is 10.

description: What will be the output of accessing last element?
explanation: Array of size 3 has indices 0,1,2. So arr[2] gives last element which is 30.

description: What will be output of this loop?
explanation: Loop runs from i=0 to i<3 so it prints all elements of array.

description: What happens if you access arr[5] in array of size 3?
explanation: Accessing out of bounds index leads to undefined behavior and may produce garbage value.

description: What is the size of array int arr[5]?
explanation: Each int is 4 bytes typically so 5 multiplied by 4 equals 20 bytes.

description: What will be output after modifying element?
explanation: arr[0] is updated to 5 so printing it gives 5.

description: What will be output of partial initialization?
explanation: Remaining elements are initialized to 0 in partial initialization.

description: What will be output of summing array?
explanation: Sum of 1 plus 2 plus 3 equals 6.

description: What will be output of this index?
explanation: arr[1] refers to second element which is 2.

description: What will be output when using sizeof on array?
explanation: Array has 3 integers each of 4 bytes so total size is 12 bytes.

description: What will be the output of the following code involving pointer arithmetic on arrays?
explanation: arr decays to a pointer to the first element. Adding 1 moves to the next element, so *(arr+1) gives 20.

description: What will be the output when using array name as pointer?
explanation: The array name represents the base address of the array, so printing it gives an address.

description: What will be output of reverse indexing trick?
explanation: In C++, a[b] is equivalent to *(a+b). So 2[arr] means *(2+arr) which is arr[2] giving 3.

description: What will be output of partial initialization with zero filling?
explanation: Remaining elements after partial initialization are set to zero.

description: What will be the output of this loop with decreasing index?
explanation: Loop starts from index 2 and decrements to 0, printing elements in reverse order.

description: What will be output when using sizeof inside loop?
explanation: sizeof(arr) gives total bytes. Dividing by sizeof(int) gives number of elements which is 3.

description: What will be output after modifying via pointer?
explanation: Pointer modifies the first element of array. So arr[0] becomes 5.

description: What will be output when accessing *(p+2)?
explanation: Pointer arithmetic moves to index 2, so value is 30.

description: What will be output of comparing array pointers?
explanation: Both pointers point to same memory so comparison is true giving 1.

description: What will be output using pointer subtraction?
explanation: Pointer subtraction gives difference in elements. p2 minus p1 equals 2 elements apart.

description: What will be the output considering array to pointer decay inside function?
explanation: When an array is passed to a function, it decays into a pointer. sizeof(arr) inside function returns size of pointer, typically 4 or 8 bytes depending on system. Here expected answer is pointer size.

description: What will be output of modifying array through pointer and index mix?
explanation: Pointer p points to arr. p[0] modifies arr[0] and *(p+1) modifies arr[1]. So array becomes 5 6 3.

description: What will be output of multi dimensional array access?
explanation: arr[1][1] accesses second row second column. Value is 4.

description: What will be output of this pointer arithmetic on 2D array?
explanation: arr is pointer to array of 2 elements. arr+1 moves to next row. *(arr+1) gives second row, and *(arr+1)+0 gives first element of second row which is 3.

description: What will be output of this tricky indexing?
explanation: Expression *(arr+1) is equivalent to arr[1], so output is 2.

description: What will be output when modifying array using reference?
explanation: Reference binds directly to arr[0], so modifying r updates original array value to 10.

description: What will be output of array name in sizeof vs pointer?
explanation: sizeof(arr) gives total array size which is 12 bytes. Pointer p size is typically 4 bytes.

description: What will be output of this loop modifying array in place?
explanation: Each element is incremented by 1 inside loop so array becomes 2 3 4.

description: What will be output of accessing *(arr+3) in array of size 3?
explanation: Accessing arr+3 goes out of bounds leading to undefined behavior and likely garbage value.

description: What will be output of this complex pointer expression?
explanation: arr is base pointer. *(arr+2) accesses third element which is 3.

description: What will be the output of the following code that reverses a string using reverse function?
explanation: The reverse function reverses the characters of the string from beginning to end, so hello becomes olleh.

description: What will be the output of substr(2,3) applied to string program?
explanation: substr starts at index 2 which is g and takes 3 characters resulting ogr.

description: How many vowels will be counted in the string education using a loop that checks a e i o u?
explanation: education contains vowels e u a i o which totals 5 vowels.

description: What will be output if characters of string code are printed using for loop from last to first?
explanation: Printing characters from last index to zero prints reversed string edoc.

description: What will be the result if string abcabc is processed to count occurrences of character a?
explanation: Character a appears twice in abcabc so count equals 2.

description: What happens if erase(1,2) is applied to string table?
explanation: erase removes two characters starting from index 1 so characters a and b are removed giving tle.

description: What will be output if find function searches for cat in string concatenate?
explanation: Substring cat begins at index 3 in concatenate.

description: Which string will be formed if characters of data are converted to uppercase?
explanation: Uppercase conversion changes all lowercase letters to uppercase producing DATA.

description: What will be the output if characters of abcde are printed skipping index 2?
explanation: Skipping index 2 removes character c resulting abde.

description: What will be the output after inserting X at position 2 in string hello?
explanation: Insert at index 2 places X after e forming heXllo.

description: What will be the output when printing the first node value?
explanation: The head pointer points to the first node. Accessing head->data prints the value stored in the first node which is 10.

description: What will be output when traversing single node linked list?
explanation: There is only one node, so loop prints its value once and stops when next becomes NULL.

description: What does a NULL next pointer indicate?
explanation: A NULL next pointer means there is no next node, so it is the last node of the linked list.

description: What will be output after creating two linked nodes?
explanation: First node points to second node. Traversal prints 10 then 20.

description: Which operator is used to access members via pointer?
explanation: Arrow operator is used to access members of a structure through pointer.

description: What will be output of accessing second node?
explanation: head->next points to second node which has value 20.

description: What will happen if head is NULL and we try to access head->data?
explanation: Accessing members of NULL pointer leads to segmentation fault which is runtime error.

description: What will be output after inserting at beginning?
explanation: New node 20 is inserted before head, so it becomes new head followed by 10.

description: What will be output after inserting at end?
explanation: New node is added after last node, so traversal prints 10 then 20.

description: What is the time complexity of inserting at beginning in linked list?
explanation: Insertion at beginning only updates pointers, so it takes constant time O(1).

description: What will be the output of the following code that prints the length of a string variable hello?
explanation: The string hello contains five characters so length function returns 5.

description: Which function is used to get the number of characters in a C plus plus string?
explanation: The length function returns the number of characters stored in the string.

description: What is the starting index position of characters in a string in C plus plus?
explanation: Strings use zero based indexing so the first index starts at position 0.

description: What will be printed when accessing index 1 of string abc?
explanation: Index 1 refers to the second character which is b.

description: Which header file must be included to use string class in C plus plus?
explanation: The string header file defines the string class used in C plus plus.

description: What happens when two strings hi and there are joined using plus operator?
explanation: Plus operator joins two strings together forming hithere.

description: What will be printed when the string world is output using cout?
explanation: Printing a string outputs its stored value which is world.

description: Which function adds a character at the end of a string?
explanation: push_back adds a single character at the end of the string.

description: What does empty function check in a string?
explanation: empty returns true when the string has no characters.

description: What will be output when printing the size of string cat?
explanation: The string cat contains three characters so size returns 3.

description: What will be the output of the following traversal when a node is skipped intentionally?
explanation: Inside the loop temp moves two steps ahead using temp = temp->next->next, so it skips every alternate node. Starting from 10, next printed is 30.

description: What will be the output when reversing pointers manually for first two nodes?
explanation: The next pointer of second node is made to point to first node, effectively reversing first two nodes. Head is updated to second node.

description: What will be output when deleting first node and printing list?
explanation: Head is moved to next node and original first node is deleted. Remaining list contains only 20.

description: What will be output when inserting in middle?
explanation: New node 15 is inserted between 10 and 20 by adjusting pointers.

description: What will be output when traversing using recursion?
explanation: Function prints data first then calls itself for next node, resulting in forward traversal.

description: What will be output when counting nodes?
explanation: Loop traverses through two nodes so count becomes 2.

description: What will be output when last node points to itself?
explanation: The node points to itself, so traversal never reaches NULL leading to infinite loop.

description: What will be output when finding second node using pointer movement?
explanation: head->next points to second node, so accessing its data gives 20.

description: What will be output when deleting last node?
explanation: The next pointer of first node is set to NULL after deleting second node, so only 10 remains.

description: What will be output when accessing next of last node?
explanation: The next pointer of last node is NULL indicating end of list.

description: What will be the output when reversing the entire linked list iteratively?
explanation: Pointers are reversed one by one. prev becomes new head. Final list order becomes reversed.

description: What will be output when detecting cycle using Floyd algorithm?
explanation: Fast and slow pointers meet if cycle exists. Since cycle is created, condition becomes true and prints 1.

description: What will be output when deleting node without head pointer?
explanation: Node to delete copies data from next node and bypasses it. So 20 is replaced by 30 and final list is 10 30.

description: What will be output when finding middle using slow fast pointers?
explanation: Slow moves one step and fast moves two steps. When fast reaches end, slow is at middle which is 30.

description: What will be output when merging two sorted linked lists manually?
explanation: Nodes are picked in sorted order from both lists resulting in merged sorted sequence.

description: What will be output when reversing using recursion?
explanation: Recursive calls reverse links and last node becomes new head.

description: What will be output when removing duplicates from sorted list?
explanation: Duplicate nodes are removed by skipping nodes with same value.

description: What will be output when checking palindrome using reverse half?
explanation: The list 1 2 1 is palindrome so comparison succeeds and prints 1.

description: What will be output when two pointers meet at intersection node?
explanation: Both lists share node with value 30 so intersection point is 30.

description: What will be output when alternating nodes are deleted?
explanation: Every alternate node is skipped so list becomes 1 3.

description: What will be the output when the following code removes consecutive duplicate characters from the string aaabbc?
explanation: The logic keeps the first occurrence and removes consecutive duplicates, resulting in abc.

description: What will be output if characters are counted where each character appears only once in string banana?
explanation: In banana, b appears once, a appears three times, n appears twice. Only b appears once.

description: What will be output if the string abcabcabc is compressed by counting consecutive characters?
explanation: Since characters repeat non consecutively, each appears once consecutively giving a1b1c1 repeated.

description: What will be output after rotating string abcde left by two positions?
explanation: Left rotation moves first two characters to the end resulting in cdeab.

description: What will be output when the following code counts distinct characters in string mississippi?
explanation: Distinct characters are m, i, s, p which totals 4 unique characters.

description: What will be output when string abc is repeated three times using loop concatenation?
explanation: Repeating abc three times results in abcabcabc.

description: What will be output if substring after removing first and last characters of string coding is printed?
explanation: Removing first c and last g leaves odin.

description: What will be output if characters of string level are checked for palindrome logic?
explanation: The string level reads same forward and backward so it is palindrome.

description: What will be output after removing all vowels from string education?
explanation: Removing vowels e u a i o leaves consonants d c t n.

description: What will be output when characters are sorted alphabetically in string zebra?
explanation: Sorting characters alphabetically arranges them as a b e r z forming aberz.

description: What will be the output of accessing root node value?
explanation: Root node stores value 10 and is directly accessed.

description: Which traversal prints root first?
explanation: Preorder traversal follows root left right, so root is printed first.

description: What is a leaf node?
explanation: Leaf node is a node that has no left or right child.

description: What is the maximum number of children in a binary tree node?
explanation: In a binary tree, each node can have at most two children.

description: What traversal is Left Root Right?
explanation: Inorder traversal follows left subtree then root then right subtree.

description: What traversal is Left Right Root?
explanation: Postorder traversal processes left subtree, then right subtree, then root.

description: What is height of a tree with single node?
explanation: Height is number of edges or levels depending on definition. Standard interview definition considers height of single node as 1.

description: Which traversal uses queue?
explanation: Level order traversal uses queue for breadth first traversal.

description: What is root node?
explanation: Root is the topmost node of tree.

description: What is time complexity of traversing all nodes?
explanation: Each node is visited once so complexity is linear O(n).

description: What is a full binary tree?
explanation: Full binary tree means each node has either 0 or 2 children.

description: What is complete binary tree?

options.text: All levels filled except last and left aligned

explanation: Complete binary tree has all levels filled except possibly last, filled left to right.

description: What is depth of root node?
explanation: Root node is at depth 0.

description: What is the main component of a graph that represents an object or node?
explanation: A vertex represents a node or object in a graph.

description: What does an edge represent in a graph?
explanation: An edge represents a connection between two vertices in a graph.

description: In an undirected graph, how many directions does an edge have?
explanation: Undirected edges have no direction meaning they work both ways.

description: What type of graph has edges with direction?
explanation: Directed graphs have edges that point from one vertex to another.

description: What is the maximum number of edges in a simple undirected graph with 3 vertices?
explanation: Maximum edges in undirected graph is n(n minus 1)/2 which gives 3 edges.

description: Which representation uses a two dimensional array to store graph connections?
explanation: Adjacency matrix uses a 2D array to represent edges between vertices.

description: Which graph representation stores neighbors using lists?
explanation: Adjacency list stores connected vertices using lists.

description: What is called when an edge connects a vertex to itself?
explanation: A self connection from vertex to itself is called a loop.

description: What is the degree of a vertex in an undirected graph?

options.text: Number of edges connected to it

explanation: Degree of vertex equals number of edges connected to that vertex.

description: What is a path in a graph?

options.text: Sequence of vertices connected by edges

explanation: A path is a sequence of vertices connected through edges.

description: What is the number of edges in a tree with 1 node?
explanation: A single node has no edges connecting it to other nodes.

description: What is the maximum number of nodes at level l in a binary tree?
explanation: At level l, maximum nodes possible are 2 raised to power l.

description: Which traversal is used to copy a tree?
explanation: Preorder traversal is useful because root is processed before children, helping in copying structure.

description: What is the number of nodes in a full binary tree of height h?
explanation: A full binary tree has maximum nodes equal to 2 power h minus 1.

description: What is degree of a node in binary tree?
explanation: Degree is defined as number of children a node has.

description: What is height of empty tree?
explanation: By convention, height of empty tree is -1.

description: What is a skewed tree?

options.text: Tree with only one child per node

explanation: In skewed tree, each node has only one child forming a chain.

description: Which data structure is used in DFS traversal?
explanation: DFS uses stack either explicitly or via recursion.

description: Which data structure is used in BFS traversal?
explanation: BFS uses queue to process nodes level by level.

description: What is minimum number of nodes in binary tree of height h?
explanation: Minimum nodes in binary tree of height h is h+1 (skewed tree).

description: What is a cycle in a graph?

options.text: A path that starts and ends at same vertex

explanation: A cycle is formed when a path starts and ends at the same vertex without repeating edges.

description: What is called a graph where every pair of vertices is connected by an edge?
explanation: In a complete graph every vertex is connected to every other vertex.

description: How many edges does a tree with n vertices contain?
explanation: A tree with n vertices always has n minus 1 edges.

description: What type of graph has weights assigned to edges?
explanation: Weighted graphs assign values or costs to edges.

description: Which traversal technique uses queue data structure?
explanation: Breadth First Search uses queue to explore vertices level by level.

description: Which traversal technique uses stack or recursion?
explanation: Depth First Search uses stack or recursion to explore deeper nodes first.

description: What is a graph with no edges called?
explanation: A graph without edges is called a null graph.

description: What is called when all vertices are reachable from every other vertex?
explanation: A connected graph allows traversal between every pair of vertices.

description: What is the degree of each vertex in a complete graph with 4 vertices?
explanation: Each vertex connects to remaining 3 vertices in complete graph with 4 vertices.

description: How many edges are present in a complete graph with 4 vertices?
explanation: Complete graph edges equal n times n minus 1 divided by 2 which gives 6.

description: What is the minimum number of edges required to connect 5 vertices?
explanation: Minimum edges required to connect n vertices is n minus 1.

description: What is the term for visiting all vertices exactly once?
explanation: Traversal refers to visiting all vertices in graph.

description: Which graph representation is memory efficient for sparse graphs?
explanation: Adjacency list saves memory when number of edges is small.

description: What is the number of vertices in a graph with adjacency matrix of size 5 by 5?
explanation: Adjacency matrix size n by n represents n vertices.

description: What does value 1 represent in adjacency matrix of unweighted graph?
explanation: Value 1 indicates that edge exists between vertices.

description: Which traversal is useful for finding shortest path in unweighted graph?
explanation: BFS explores level wise and finds shortest path in unweighted graph.

description: What is called a graph that contains at least one cycle?
explanation: Graph containing at least one cycle is cyclic graph.

description: What is called a graph with no cycles?
explanation: Graph without any cycle is called acyclic graph.

description: Which data structure is commonly used to implement BFS?
explanation: Queue ensures FIFO order used in BFS traversal.

description: What is the total number of nodes in a perfect binary tree of height 2?
explanation: Total nodes in perfect binary tree is 2 power h plus 1 minus 1. For height 2 it becomes 2 power 3 minus 1 which is 7.

description: Which node has no parent?
explanation: Root node is the only node that has no parent.

description: What is the maximum number of leaf nodes in a binary tree of height h?
explanation: Maximum leaf nodes in binary tree is 2 power h.

description: What is the number of nodes in a tree with no edges?
explanation: A tree with no edges consists of a single node.

description: Which traversal is also called Depth First Search?

options.text: DFS traversals like inorder preorder postorder

explanation: DFS includes inorder preorder and postorder traversals.

description: Which traversal visits nodes level by level?
explanation: Level order traversal visits nodes level by level using queue.

description: What is number of children of leaf node?
explanation: Leaf node has no children.

description: Which node has at least one child?
explanation: Internal nodes have at least one child.

description: What is the minimum height of binary tree with n nodes?
explanation: Minimum height occurs in balanced tree and is proportional to log n.

description: What is the maximum height of binary tree with n nodes?
explanation: Maximum height occurs in skewed tree and equals n.

description: What will be the output of inorder traversal for BST?
explanation: Inorder traversal of BST always gives sorted sequence.

description: What is time complexity of searching in balanced BST?
explanation: Balanced BST halves search space at each step so time is logarithmic.

description: What will be output of height calculation for given tree?
explanation: Height is number of levels. Given tree has 3 levels.

description: Which traversal uses stack explicitly?
explanation: DFS iterative approach uses stack explicitly.

description: What is diameter of tree?
explanation: Diameter is longest path between any two nodes.

description: What is LCA?
explanation: LCA means lowest common ancestor of two nodes.

description: What is condition for BST?
explanation: BST property is left subtree values less than root and right greater.

description: Which traversal is used to delete tree?
explanation: Postorder ensures children are deleted before parent.

description: What is maximum nodes in binary tree of height h?
explanation: Maximum nodes formula is 2 power h minus 1.

description: Which structure is used in level order traversal?
explanation: Queue is used in BFS traversal.

description: What is time complexity of inserting into BST on average?
explanation: In average case BST remains balanced so insertion takes logarithmic time.

description: What happens in worst case BST search?
explanation: If BST becomes skewed, search degrades to linear time.

description: Which traversal gives sorted order in BST?
explanation: Inorder traversal prints elements in sorted order in BST.

description: What is number of edges in tree with n nodes?
explanation: A tree with n nodes always has n minus 1 edges.

description: Which traversal is best for expression tree evaluation?
explanation: Postorder traversal evaluates expression trees correctly.

description: What is the space complexity of recursion in tree traversal?
explanation: Recursive calls take space proportional to height, worst case O(n).

description: Which node is deepest in tree?
explanation: Leaf nodes at maximum depth are deepest.

description: What is property of balanced tree?
explanation: Balanced tree ensures height difference between subtrees is at most 1.

description: Which structure is used to store recursion calls?
explanation: Recursion uses call stack internally.

description: Which traversal visits nodes from top to bottom?
explanation: BFS or level order traversal visits nodes level by level.

description: What is the maximum number of nodes in a binary tree of height 3?
explanation: Maximum nodes formula is 2 power h plus 1 minus 1. For height 3 it is 2 power 4 minus 1 which equals 15.

description: What is the minimum number of nodes in a binary tree of height h?
explanation: Minimum nodes occur in skewed tree and equal h plus 1.

description: What is the relation between nodes and edges in a tree?
explanation: A tree with n nodes always has n minus 1 edges.

description: Which traversal is used to clone a tree structure?
explanation: Preorder helps recreate structure since root is processed before children.

description: Which case leads to worst case height in BST?
explanation: Sorted input creates skewed BST resulting in worst case height.

description: What is the height of a complete binary tree with n nodes approximately?
explanation: Height of complete binary tree is logarithmic.

description: Which node is called ancestor?
explanation: Ancestor is any node on path from root to given node.

description: What is subtree?

options.text: Tree formed by node and its descendants

explanation: Subtree consists of node and all its children recursively.

description: What is sibling node?
explanation: Siblings share the same parent node.

description: Which traversal is used in heap construction?
explanation: Heap uses level order which is BFS.

description: What will be the BFS traversal starting from vertex 0 for adjacency list where 0 connects to 1 and 2?
explanation: BFS visits neighbors level wise so starting at 0 visits 1 then 2.

description: How many edges are present in an undirected graph if sum of all vertex degrees is 12?
explanation: Sum of degrees equals two times number of edges so edges equal 12 divided by 2 equals 6.

description: What will be the DFS traversal starting from 0 if adjacency is 0 connects to 1 then 2?
explanation: DFS follows first neighbor fully before moving to next so result is 0 1 2.

description: What is the number of edges in complete graph with 5 vertices?
explanation: Number of edges equals n times n minus 1 divided by 2 so result is 5 times 4 divided by 2 equals 10.

description: What is the number of vertices in graph having adjacency matrix of size 6 by 6?
explanation: Matrix size n by n represents n vertices so 6 vertices exist.

description: What will be BFS traversal order if graph edges are 0 to 1, 0 to 2, 1 to 3?
explanation: BFS visits 0 first then neighbors 1 and 2 then vertex 3.

description: What is the degree of each vertex in a complete graph of 6 vertices?
explanation: Each vertex connects to remaining n minus 1 vertices so degree is 5.

description: Which condition confirms that undirected graph contains cycle?
explanation: If edges exceed vertices minus 1 then at least one cycle exists.

description: How many edges exist in tree having 8 vertices?
explanation: Tree with n vertices always contains n minus 1 edges.

description: What is maximum number of edges in directed graph with 4 vertices?
explanation: Maximum edges in directed graph equals n times n minus 1 so 4 times 3 equals 12.

explanation: Inorder traversal of BST gives sorted sequence.

description: What is time complexity of finding diameter using naive approach?
explanation: Naive diameter computation recalculates height for each node leading to O(n^2).

description: What is optimized time complexity of diameter calculation?
explanation: Using single traversal we can compute diameter in O(n).

description: What will be output of level order traversal?
explanation: Level order prints nodes level by level from left to right.

description: What is time complexity of building BST from sorted array?
explanation: Balanced BST can be constructed in linear time.

description: What is time complexity of LCA in BST?
explanation: In BST we can traverse down using properties giving O(log n).

description: What is the condition for AVL tree?
explanation: AVL tree maintains balance factor difference at most 1.

description: What is time complexity of AVL insertion?
explanation: AVL maintains balance so insertion remains logarithmic.

description: Which traversal is used for serialization?
explanation: Preorder is commonly used to serialize trees.

description: What is maximum nodes in AVL tree of height h?
explanation: AVL nodes follow Fibonacci-like relation.

description: What will be BFS traversal starting from 0 in graph with edges 0-1, 0-2, 1-3, 2-4?
explanation: BFS visits neighbors level wise starting with 0 then 1 and 2 then 3 and 4.

description: What will be DFS traversal starting from 0 if adjacency is 0 connects to 1 and 2, and 1 connects to 3?
explanation: DFS follows first branch completely before moving to next branch.

description: How many connected components exist in graph having vertices 0-1 connected and 2-3 connected?
explanation: Two separate groups exist so two connected components are present.

description: What is the number of edges in complete graph with 7 vertices?
explanation: Edges equal n times n minus 1 divided by 2 so 7 times 6 divided by 2 equals 21.

description: If adjacency matrix contains symmetric values across diagonal, what type of graph is represented?
explanation: Symmetric matrix indicates undirected graph because edges exist both ways.

description: What is maximum number of edges in directed graph with 5 vertices?
explanation: Maximum edges equal n times n minus 1 so 5 times 4 equals 20.

description: What happens if DFS is applied on disconnected graph without restarting traversal?
explanation: DFS will only visit vertices reachable from starting node.

description: What is minimum number of edges required to make graph with 9 vertices connected?
explanation: Minimum edges equal vertices minus 1 which equals 8.

description: Which traversal method guarantees shortest path in unweighted graph?
explanation: BFS guarantees shortest path in unweighted graph.

description: If graph contains V vertices and E edges and E is less than V minus 1, what type of graph exists?
explanation: Graph with fewer than V minus 1 edges cannot be connected.

description: What is time complexity of validating a BST?
explanation: We need to visit every node once to validate BST property so complexity is O(n).

description: What is time complexity of finding kth smallest in BST using inorder?
explanation: In inorder traversal we may traverse k elements to find kth smallest so O(k).

description: What is the space complexity of recursion in balanced tree traversal?
explanation: Balanced tree has height log n so recursion stack is O(log n).

description: What is space complexity of BFS traversal?
explanation: Queue may store up to n nodes in worst case so O(n).

description: What is the worst case height of AVL tree?
explanation: AVL tree maintains balance so height remains logarithmic.

description: Which traversal is used to convert tree to linked list?
explanation: Inorder traversal can convert BST to sorted linked list.

description: What is the time complexity of converting sorted array to balanced BST?
explanation: Each element is processed once so complexity is linear.

description: Which algorithm is used to balance BST?
explanation: AVL tree uses rotations to maintain balance.

description: What is complexity of finding LCA in binary tree (not BST)?
explanation: We may need to traverse entire tree so complexity is O(n).

description: What is the complexity of checking if tree is balanced?
explanation: Optimized solution checks height and balance in single traversal O(n).

description: What is the time complexity of constructing a tree from inorder and preorder traversals?
explanation: Using a hashmap for inorder indices allows construction in linear time.

description: What is the complexity of Morris traversal?
explanation: Morris traversal visits each node constant times so overall O(n).

description: What is the space complexity of Morris traversal?
explanation: Morris traversal uses no extra space, only constant variables.

description: What is the complexity of flattening binary tree to linked list?
explanation: Each node is visited once so time complexity is linear.

description: Which traversal is used to find kth largest element in BST efficiently?
explanation: Reverse inorder gives elements in descending order.

description: What is time complexity of finding maximum path sum?
explanation: Each node is visited once while calculating path sums.

description: What is the complexity of vertical order traversal?
explanation: Using map structure leads to log factor, overall O(n log n).

description: What is time complexity of serialization and deserialization?
explanation: Each node is processed once so complexity is O(n).

description: What is the complexity of checking symmetry of tree?
explanation: Each node is compared once so complexity is O(n).

description: What is time complexity of converting binary tree to doubly linked list?
explanation: Each node is processed once so conversion is linear time.

description: If a graph has 6 vertices and 15 edges, what type of graph is it?
explanation: Maximum edges in undirected graph with 6 vertices is 6 times 5 divided by 2 equals 15 so it is complete graph.

explanation: Directed complete graph contains n times n minus 1 edges so 6 times 5 equals 30.

explanation: Edges in bipartite graph equal product of partition sizes so 3 times 4 equals 12.

explanation: Tree contains exactly one path between every pair and no cycles.

explanation: Sum of degrees equals 8 times 3 equals 24 so edges equal 24 divided by 2 equals 12.

explanation: Each vertex degree equals n minus 1 so degree sum equals n times n minus 1.

explanation: Each edge counted twice in adjacency matrix so divide by 2.

explanation: Array is reversed using swapping so output is 3 2 1.

description: What will be output of intersection of arrays?
explanation: Common element is 2.

description: Which keyword is used to define a class in Java?
explanation: Class keyword is used to define a class in Java.

description: Which method is starting point of Java program?
explanation: Main method is entry point from where Java program execution begins.

description: Which symbol is used to end statement in Java?
explanation: Semicolon is used to terminate every Java statement.

description: Which keyword is used to create object in Java?
explanation: New keyword creates object by allocating memory.

description: Which keyword is used to display output in Java?
explanation: System.out.println() is commonly used to print output to console.

explanation: Int data type is used to store whole numbers.

explanation: Boolean data type stores only true or false values.

explanation: Char data type stores single character value.

description: Which keyword is used to make variable constant?
explanation: Final keyword prevents variable value from changing.

description: Which loop repeats code multiple times?
explanation: For loop is commonly used to repeat block of code multiple times.

explanation: If statement is used to check condition and take decision based on it.

explanation: Plus operator (+) is used to perform addition.

description: Which operator is used to subtract numbers?
explanation: Minus operator (-) subtracts one number from another.

description: Which loop is used when number of iterations is known?
explanation: For loop is commonly used when number of repetitions is known.

explanation: Double slash (//) is used to write single-line comments.

description: Which keyword is used to skip current loop iteration?

description: Which keyword is used to return value from method?

description: Which data type is used to store decimal values?

description: Which keyword is used to import classes?

description: Which keyword refers to current object?
explanation: This keyword refers to current object instance.

description: Which keyword refers to parent class?
explanation: Super keyword is used to refer parent class members.

description: Which access modifier allows access everywhere?
explanation: Public members can be accessed from anywhere.

description: Which keyword is used to define interface?
explanation: Interface keyword is used to create interface in Java.

description: Which keyword is used to inherit class?
explanation: Extends keyword is used for class inheritance.

description: What is time complexity of finding pair with given sum using hashing?
explanation: Using hashmap we can find pair in single pass so O(n).

description: What is time complexity of two pointer approach on sorted array?
explanation: Two pointers move linearly so complexity is O(n).

description: What is time complexity of sliding window maximum using deque?
explanation: Each element is added and removed once so O(n).

description: What is time complexity of finding majority element using Moore voting?
explanation: Moore voting scans array once so O(n).

description: What is time complexity of longest increasing subsequence using DP?
explanation: DP solution uses nested loops leading to O(n^2).

description: What is optimized complexity of LIS using binary search?
explanation: Using binary search LIS can be solved in O(n log n).

description: What is time complexity of finding median of two sorted arrays (optimal)?
explanation: Binary search on smaller array gives O(log(min(n,m))).

description: What is time complexity of trapping rain water using two pointers?
explanation: Two pointer approach scans array once so O(n).

description: What is time complexity of maximum product subarray?
explanation: We track max and min product in single pass so O(n).

description: What is time complexity of finding subarray with given sum using hashmap?
explanation: Hashmap allows prefix sum lookup in O(1) per element so total O(n).

description: What will be output of Kadane algorithm on this array?
explanation: Maximum subarray sum is 7 using Kadane algorithm.

description: What is time complexity of Kadane algorithm?
explanation: Kadane algorithm runs in linear time.

description: What is time complexity of merge sort on array?
explanation: Merge sort divides and merges so complexity is O(n log n).

description: What is time complexity of quicksort average case?
explanation: Average case quicksort is O(n log n).

description: What is worst case complexity of quicksort?
explanation: Worst case occurs when pivot is smallest or largest giving O(n^2).

description: What is time complexity of searching in sorted array using binary search?
explanation: Binary search halves the array each step so O(log n).

description: What is time complexity of finding all subarrays?
explanation: Number of subarrays is n*(n+1)/2 so complexity is O(n^2).

description: What is time complexity of finding all subarrays sum using brute force?
explanation: Three nested loops lead to O(n^3).

description: What is space complexity of merge sort?
explanation: Merge sort requires extra array so O(n) space.

description: What is time complexity of counting sort?
explanation: Counting sort depends on range k so complexity is O(n+k).

description: Which class is used to create strings in Java?

explanation: Length() method returns number of characters in string.

explanation: contains() checks whether substring is present.

description: Which problems can be solved using sliding window?

options.text: Maximum sum subarray of size k

String literals are stored in String Pool, so both references point to same object.
new String() creates separate objects, so references differ.
equals() compares actual content, not references.
Strings are immutable. concat() creates new string but original remains unchanged.
Returned string from concat() is assigned back to variable.
substring(1) returns characters from index 1 till end.
substring(1,3) returns index 1 to 2.
Converted to lowercase then uppercase again.
Last character index is length()-1 which is 'a'.
Compiler optimizes literal concatenation, so same pooled object is used.
Concatenation using variable creates new object, not pooled one.

options.text: Binary search optimization O(n log n)

explanation: LIS can be solved using DP binary search optimization and brute force recursion but not purely greedy.

explanation: Prefix sum hashmap and brute force work. Sliding window works only for positive numbers.

description: Which algorithms can find kth smallest element?
explanation: Quickselect heap and sorting can find kth smallest element.

description: Which problems can be solved using two pointers technique?
explanation: Two pointers are used in pair sum trapping rain water and container problem.

description: Which data structures are useful for array optimization problems?
explanation: HashMap deque and stack are commonly used in array problems.

description: Which problems can be solved using monotonic stack?
explanation: Monotonic stack is used in NGE histogram and stock span problems.

description: Which techniques are used in range query problems?
explanation: Prefix sum segment tree and BIT are used for range queries.

description: Which approaches help optimize brute force array problems?
explanation: Hashing two pointers and sliding window optimize brute force.

description: Which problems can be solved using divide and conquer?
explanation: Merge sort quick sort and divide approach for max subarray use divide and conquer.

description: Which techniques are used in competitive programming for arrays?
explanation: Prefix sum difference array and sliding window are commonly used.

substring(start,end) with same index returns empty string.
replace() replaces all occurrences of 'A' with 'a'.
indexOf() returns -1 if character not found.
substring(0,length) returns entire string.
Concatenating empty string does not change string content.
substring(2) gives 'va', then substring(1) gives 'a'.
split(" ") splits string into two parts at space.
Substring 'av' exists inside 'Java'.
equals() is case-sensitive, so 'JAVA' is not equal to 'java'.
Uppercase conversion does not change string length.

description: What is the output of printing head data?
explanation: Head node stores the first value which is 10.
code: class Node{ int data; Node next; Node(int d){ data=d; } } class Main{ public static void main(String[] args){ Node head=new Node(10); System.out.print(head.data); } }

description: What will be output of accessing next node?
explanation: head.next points to node containing 20.
code: class Node{ int data; Node next; Node(int d){ data=d; } } class Main{ public static void main(String[] args){ Node head=new Node(10); head.next=new Node(20); System.out.print(head.next.data); } }

description: What does null in next pointer indicate?
explanation: Null in next pointer means there is no next node.

description: What is head in linked list?
explanation: Head points to the first node of linked list.

description: What is a node in linked list?
explanation: Node contains data and reference to next node.

description: What will be output of traversing list?
explanation: Traversal prints nodes sequentially from head.
code: class Node{ int data; Node next; Node(int d){ data=d; } } class Main{ public static void main(String[] args){ Node head=new Node(10); head.next=new Node(20); Node temp=head; while(temp!=null){ System.out.print(temp.data+" "); temp=temp.next; } } }

description: What is time complexity of accessing nth element?
explanation: We need to traverse from head to nth node.

description: What is time complexity of insertion at head?
explanation: Insertion at head requires constant time pointer update.

description: What is time complexity of insertion at end without tail pointer?
explanation: We need to traverse to last node so O(n).

description: What is a singly linked list?

options.text: Each node points to next only

explanation: Singly linked list nodes contain only next pointer.

explanation: Single node prints its value once.
code: class Node{ int data; Node next; Node(int d){ data=d; } } class Main{ public static void main(String[] args){ Node head=new Node(10); Node t=head; while(t!=null){ System.out.print(t.data); t=t.next; } } }

explanation: Traversal requires visiting all nodes so O(n).

explanation: Reversing links results in order 20 10.

description: What is null pointer?
explanation: Null pointer means no reference is assigned.

description: Which operation is faster in linked list compared to array?
explanation: Insertion at head is constant time in linked list.

description: What is required to traverse linked list?
explanation: Traversal is done using pointer moving node by node.

description: What will be output after reversing a linked list?
explanation: Reversing changes direction of links so list becomes 3 2 1.
code: class Node{ int data; Node next; Node(int d){ data=d; } } class Main{ static Node reverse(Node head){ Node prev=null,curr=head; while(curr!=null){ Node next=curr.next; curr.next=prev; prev=curr; curr=next; } return prev;} public static void main(String[] args){ Node head=new Node(1); head.next=new Node(2); head.next.next=new Node(3); head=reverse(head); Node t=head; while(t!=null){ System.out.print(t.data+" "); t=t.next;} } }

description: What is time complexity of reversing a linked list?
explanation: Each node is visited once so time complexity is O(n).

explanation: Sum of degrees = 2 × number of edges = 2 × 7 = 14.

explanation: Edges in complete graph = n(n-1)/2 = 5×4/2 = 10.

description: If a graph has 6 vertices and 5 edges and is connected, what type of graph is it?
explanation: Connected graph with n vertices and n-1 edges is a tree.

description: What is the number of edges in a complete bipartite graph with partitions of size 2 and 3?
explanation: Edges in complete bipartite graph = m × n = 2 × 3 = 6.

description: What is time complexity of finding subarray with given sum using hashmap?

description: What is the output of int[] arr = {1,-1,1,-1}; int sum=0,count=0; java.util.HashMap<Integer,Integer> map = new java.util.HashMap<>(); map.put(0,1); for(int i=0;i<arr.length;i++){ sum+=arr[i]; if(map.containsKey(sum)) count+=map.get(sum); map.put(sum,map.getOrDefault(sum,0)+1);} System.out.println(count);?
explanation: Counts subarrays with sum 0 → result = 4.

description: What is the output of int[] arr = {2,3,1,2,4,3}; int target=7,left=0,sum=0,minLen=Integer.MAX_VALUE; for(int right=0;right<arr.length;right++){ sum+=arr[right]; while(sum>=target){ minLen=Math.min(minLen,right-left+1); sum-=arr[left++]; }} System.out.println(minLen);?
explanation: Smallest subarray ≥7 is {4,3} → length = 2.

description: What is the output of int[] arr = {1,2,3,4}; int res=0; for(int i=0;i<arr.length;i++){ for(int j=i;j<arr.length;j++){ for(int k=i;k<=j;k++){ res += arr[k]; } } } System.out.println(res);?
explanation: Brute force sum of all subarrays → 50.

description: What is the output of int[] arr = {1,3,2,3,1}; int count=0; for(int i=0;i<arr.length;i++){ for(int j=i+1;j<arr.length;j++){ if(arr[i]>arr[j]) count++; }} System.out.println(count);?
explanation: Counts inversions → pairs: (3,2),(3,1),(2,1),(3,1) → total = 4.

description: Which algorithm is used to find number of inversions efficiently?
explanation: Modified merge sort counts inversions in O(n log n).

description: What is time complexity of inversion count using merge sort?
explanation: Merge sort divides and merges → O(n log n).

description: What is the output of int[] arr = {1,2,3,4}; int left=0,right=arr.length-1; while(left<right){ int temp=arr[left]; arr[left]=arr[right]; arr[right]=temp; left++; right--; } System.out.println(arr[1]);?
explanation: Array becomes {4,3,2,1} → arr[1]=3.

description: What is the output of int[] arr = {1,2,3}; int[] b = arr.clone(); arr[0]=10; System.out.println(b[0]);?
explanation: Clone creates separate copy → b unaffected.

description: Which technique is used in maximum water container problem?
explanation: Two pointer technique is optimal.

description: What is the output of int[] arr = {1,2,3,4}; int res=0; for(int i=0;i<arr.length;i++){ res += arr[i]*(i+1)*(arr.length-i); } System.out.println(res);?
explanation: Contribution formula for sum of all subarrays → 50.

explanation: Inorder traversal of BST returns keys in sorted order.

explanation: A connected acyclic tree always has n-1 edges.

description: Which traversal of a BST gives descending order?

explanation: Trie stores characters by prefixes efficiently.

description: What is the time complexity of searching a word of length m in a Trie?
explanation: Each character is checked once.

description: Which traversal is used to copy a tree structure exactly?
explanation: Preorder processes node before children, useful for reconstruction.

explanation: Total child pointers = 2n, real edges = n-1, nulls = 2n-(n-1)=n+1.

description: Which tree is used in databases/file systems for indexing?
explanation: B-Trees are widely used for disk-based indexing.

description: What is the diameter of a tree?

options.text: Longest path between any two nodes

explanation: Diameter measures the longest node-to-node path.

description: Which algorithm commonly computes shortest paths in an unweighted tree from root?
explanation: BFS finds shortest paths in unweighted graphs/trees.

explanation: Each insertion may traverse increasing height, totaling O(n²).

description: Which property is always true for a min-heap?
explanation: Min-heap maintains smallest key at root and parent <= children.

description: Which traversal of expression tree gives postfix expression?
explanation: Postorder outputs operands before operator (postfix).

description: What is the maximum number of edges in path from root to leaf in a perfect tree of height h?
explanation: Height is defined as maximum number of edges root to leaf.

description: Which technique finds Lowest Common Ancestor efficiently in a BST?
explanation: BST ordering lets us move left/right based on values.

explanation: Unique simple path property characterizes trees.

explanation: Recursion depth can become n in skewed tree.

description: A graph is made up of vertices and ____.
explanation: A graph consists of vertices (nodes) and edges (connections).

description: Another name for a vertex in a graph is:
explanation: Vertex is commonly called a node.

description: An edge connects two ____.
explanation: Edges represent connections between vertices.

description: A graph with edges having direction is called:
explanation: Directed graphs have arrows on edges.

description: A graph with no edge direction is called:
explanation: Undirected graph edges work both ways.

description: Which data structure is commonly used for BFS?
explanation: BFS uses FIFO queue.

description: Which data structure is commonly used for DFS?
explanation: DFS uses stack or recursion.

description: What is the maximum number of edges from one vertex in a directed graph called?
explanation: Out-degree is number of outgoing edges.

description: Number of incoming edges to a vertex is called:
explanation: In-degree counts incoming edges.

description: A graph with no cycles is called:
explanation: Acyclic means no cycles exist.

description: A connected acyclic undirected graph is a:
explanation: A tree is connected and has no cycles.

description: Which graph representation uses rows and columns?
explanation: Adjacency matrix stores connections in 2D table.

description: Which graph representation stores neighbors in lists?

description: In an undirected graph, edge (A,B) means:

options.text: A to B and B to A

explanation: Undirected edges work both directions.

explanation: Weighted graphs assign values to edges.

description: A path that starts and ends at same vertex is a:
explanation: Such closed path is called a cycle.

description: What is the degree of a vertex in an undirected graph?
explanation: Degree is number of incident edges.

description: Which graph has every pair of vertices connected?
explanation: Complete graph connects all vertex pairs.

description: If graph has many edges compared to vertices, it is called:
explanation: Dense graphs contain many edges.

description: If graph has very few edges, it is called:
explanation: Sparse graph has relatively fewer edges.

description: Which graph has no edges?
explanation: Null graph contains vertices but no edges.

description: Which algorithm can visit all reachable nodes from a start node?
explanation: Both BFS and DFS can traverse reachable nodes.

explanation: 1 commonly indicates connection present.

description: Which graph is a directed acyclic graph?
explanation: DAG stands for Directed Acyclic Graph.

description: What is the time complexity of BFS using adjacency list representation?
explanation: Each vertex and each edge is processed at most once.

description: What is the time complexity of DFS using adjacency list?
explanation: DFS visits each vertex and edge once.

description: Which algorithm is used to find shortest path in an unweighted graph?
explanation: BFS finds shortest path in terms of edges in unweighted graphs.

description: Which algorithm is commonly used for topological sorting?
explanation: Kahn's algorithm uses indegree values and queue.

description: Topological sorting is possible only for:
explanation: Topological ordering exists only for Directed Acyclic Graphs.

description: Which graph representation is more space efficient for sparse graphs?
explanation: Adjacency list stores only existing edges.

description: Which graph representation gives O(1) edge existence check?
explanation: Matrix lookup mat[u][v] is constant time.

description: In an undirected graph, sum of all vertex degrees equals:
explanation: Each edge contributes degree count to two vertices.

description: Maximum edges in a simple undirected graph with n vertices is:
explanation: Every pair of vertices can have one edge.

description: Maximum edges in a simple directed graph with n vertices is:
explanation: Each ordered pair of distinct vertices can form an edge.

explanation: Disjoint Set Union helps detect repeated connections.

description: Which algorithm finds Minimum Spanning Tree?
explanation: Both Prim's and Kruskal's are MST algorithms.

description: Minimum Spanning Tree is defined for:
explanation: MST connects all vertices with minimum total weight.

description: Which algorithm finds shortest paths with non-negative weights?
explanation: Dijkstra works for graphs with non-negative edge weights.

description: Which algorithm can handle negative edge weights (no negative cycle)?
explanation: Bellman-Ford relaxes edges repeatedly and supports negatives.

description: A connected graph with V vertices and V-1 edges is always a:
explanation: Connected graph with V-1 edges has no cycles, so it is a tree.

description: How many edges are present in any tree with V vertices?
explanation: Every tree with V vertices has exactly V-1 edges.

description: Which traversal is useful for connected components counting?
explanation: Repeated BFS/DFS from unvisited nodes counts components.

description: In BFS, vertices are visited in order of:
explanation: BFS explores nodes level by level.

description: Which graph contains at least one cycle?
explanation: A cyclic graph has one or more cycles.

description: What is the indegree of source node in a DAG topological order start?
explanation: Topological sorting starts with nodes of indegree 0.

description: Which representation is better for dense graphs?
explanation: For dense graphs, matrix is often convenient and efficient for checks.

description: How many vertices can have odd degree in an undirected graph?
explanation: Number of odd degree vertices is always even.

explanation: Heap efficiently extracts minimum distance node.

description: What is the shortest path edge count from source to itself in BFS?

description: What is the maximum number of edges in a simple undirected graph with n vertices?

description: What is the maximum number of edges in a simple directed graph with n vertices?

description: In an undirected graph, the sum of degrees of all vertices equals:

description: Number of vertices with odd degree in an undirected graph is always:

description: Which condition guarantees existence of an Euler circuit in connected undirected graph?

description: What is the time complexity of Kruskal's MST algorithm mainly due to sorting?
explanation: Sorting edges dominates runtime.

description: Which data structure is essential in Kruskal's algorithm?

description: Which algorithm grows MST from a starting vertex?

description: Cycle detection in directed graph using DFS depends on:

description: Which algorithm is commonly used for articulation points in graph?

description: Which algorithm is used to find bridges in an undirected graph?

description: What is the shortest path algorithm for all pairs with negative edges but no negative cycles?
explanation: Floyd-Warshall supports negative weights if no negative cycle exists.

description: What is the time complexity of BFS on adjacency matrix representation?
explanation: Need to scan full row of matrix for each vertex.

description: A graph is bipartite if it contains:
explanation: Undirected graph is bipartite iff it has no odd cycle.

description: Which traversal is generally used to test bipartite coloring level by level?
explanation: BFS naturally alternates levels for two-coloring.

description: What is the diameter of an unweighted tree commonly found by two BFS runs?

options.text: Longest shortest path between two nodes

explanation: Tree diameter is longest path between any two nodes.

description: In DSU with path compression, find() operation becomes nearly:
explanation: Inverse Ackermann complexity is practically constant.

description: Which graph problem is solved by Kahn's algorithm failure to process all nodes?
explanation: If nodes remain unprocessed, directed cycle exists.

description: What is the number of edges in a complete graph K_n?

description: If all edge weights are equal, shortest path can be found efficiently using:
explanation: Equal weights reduce to unweighted shortest path problem.

description: What is the minimum number of edges required to keep n vertices connected?

description: What is a Linked List?

options.text: A collection of nodes connected using pointers

explanation: A linked list is a linear data structure where elements (nodes) are connected using pointers.

description: Which part of a node stores the address of next node?
explanation: The next pointer stores the address of the next node.

description: Which pointer stores the first node of linked list?
explanation: Head points to the first node of the linked list.

description: What does NULL in last node's next indicate?
explanation: The last node points to NULL indicating no further node exists.

description: Which operation is efficient in linked list compared to array?
explanation: Insertion at beginning is O(1) in linked list.

description: Which symbol is used to access members through pointer in C++?
explanation: Arrow operator is used to access structure/class members using pointer.

description: How many pointers are generally present in singly linked list node?
explanation: A singly linked list node usually contains one next pointer.

description: What is the time complexity to insert at head?
explanation: Only head pointer update is needed.

description: Which traversal starts from head node?
explanation: Traversal is done sequentially from head node to NULL.

description: Which node comes after head?
explanation: The next node after head is head->next.

description: What is the main disadvantage of linked list?
explanation: Each node stores pointer(s), requiring extra memory.

description: What happens if head is NULL?
explanation: NULL head means no node in the list.

description: Which keyword allocates memory dynamically in C++?
explanation: new is commonly used in C++ to create linked list nodes.

description: Which pointer is used for traversal?
explanation: A temporary pointer is commonly used to traverse nodes.

description: Which of these supports dynamic size?
explanation: Linked lists can grow or shrink dynamically.

description: What is stored in data part of node?
explanation: Data field stores the actual element value.

description: Which operation removes first node?
explanation: Deleting head removes the first node.

description: What is the time complexity of traversing n nodes?
explanation: Each node is visited once.

description: Which node points to NULL in singly linked list?
explanation: The last node's next pointer is NULL.

description: Which is not possible directly in linked list?
explanation: Direct indexing like arr[i] is not supported efficiently.

description: Which pointer variable usually stores last node?
explanation: Tail commonly stores address of last node.

description: What does temp=temp->next do?
explanation: It advances traversal pointer to next node.

description: How many head nodes are generally used in one simple linked list?
explanation: Normally one head pointer stores first node.

description: Which data structure uses nodes and links?
explanation: Linked lists are made of nodes connected by links.

description: Which statement creates a node pointer?
explanation: Node* declares a pointer to Node.

description: What is needed to visit every node?
explanation: Traversal means visiting nodes one by one.

description: Which memory is commonly used for linked list nodes?
explanation: Nodes are commonly dynamically allocated in heap memory.

description: Which operation adds node at end?
explanation: Appending means insertion at the end.

description: Which field changes after inserting at head?
explanation: Head must point to the new first node.

description: Which condition is used while traversing till end?
explanation: Traversal continues until pointer becomes NULL.

description: Which structure can grow during runtime?
explanation: Linked lists can dynamically expand at runtime.

description: What is the time complexity of inserting a node at the end of a singly linked list when tail pointer is available?
explanation: With a tail pointer, the last node is directly accessible, so insertion at end takes constant time.

description: What is the time complexity to search an element in an unsorted singly linked list of n nodes?
explanation: You may need to traverse all nodes in worst case.

description: Which pointer must be updated first while inserting a node at beginning?
explanation: First connect new node to current head, then move head.

description: What is the result of deleting head node from list 10->20->30?
explanation: After deleting first node, head moves to second node.

description: Which linked list allows traversal in both forward and backward direction?

description: What is a linked list mainly made of?

explanation: A basic node stores value + pointer to next node.

explanation: head points to the first node.

explanation: Only a few pointer updates are needed.

explanation: Need to move node by node to reach index.

explanation: Second node becomes new head.

explanation: Doubly linked list has next and prev.

explanation: Structural changes are efficient.
